name: UDID Card (Unique Disability ID)
description: The UDID Card is a Unique Disability Identity Card issued by the Ministry of Social Justice & Empowerment. It creates a standardised national database and enables seamless access to all disability benefits through a single card.
benefits: Single-point identification for all government disability benefits
Eliminates need for multiple disability certificates
Digital health and disability profile
Accepted across all government schemes nationwide

name: ADIP Scheme – Assistive Devices for PwD
description: The Assistance to Disabled Persons (ADIP) Scheme provides free or subsidised assistive devices to PwDs to promote physical, social and psychological rehabilitation.
benefits: Free or subsidised wheelchairs, hearing aids, crutches, white canes, Braille kits
Battery-operated wheelchairs for severe locomotor disabilities
Assistive devices provided through government hospitals and NGO camps
Support for fitting of artificial limbs

name: Pre-Matric Scholarship for Students with Disabilities
description: Scholarship for students with disabilities studying in Classes 9 and 10 to reduce dropout and encourage continuation of education. Administered by DEPwD.
benefits: ₹700/month for day scholars, ₹1,100/month for hostellers
Annual book grant of ₹1,000
Ad-hoc grant of ₹2,000 per annum for incidentals
Examination fee reimbursement

name: Niramaya Health Insurance Scheme
description: Health insurance scheme for persons with Autism, Cerebral Palsy, Mental Retardation, and Multiple Disabilities, implemented by the National Trust under the Ministry of Social Justice.
benefits: Health insurance cover up to ₹1 lakh per annum
OPD coverage up to ₹15,000 per year
Annual premium of only ₹500 (₹250 for BPL families)
Coverage for hospitalization, surgeries, and alternative therapies
Transportation charges for medical visits covered

name: National Fellowship for Persons with Disabilities
description: UGC-administered fellowship funded by DEPwD providing financial assistance to PwD students pursuing M.Phil & Ph.D to increase research opportunities and promote inclusion in academia.
benefits: JRF: ₹31,000/month for first two years
SRF: ₹35,000/month for remaining tenure
HRA as per university norms
Annual contingency grant ₹10,000 (JRF) / ₹20,500 (SRF)
200 fellowship slots available per year

name: Post-Matric Scholarship for Students with Disabilities
description: Financial assistance for students with disabilities pursuing post-matriculation studies (Class 11 onwards up to Ph.D). Covers tuition, maintenance, and other allowances.
benefits: Maintenance allowance ranging from ₹1,200 to ₹1,700/month
Full reimbursement of compulsory non-refundable fees
Study tour charges up to ₹1,600 per annum
Book allowance up to ₹1,200 per annum
Thesis typing & printing charges

name: PM Awas Yojana – Priority for PwD
description: Under Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana (Gramin & Urban), Persons with Disabilities are given priority allotment of houses with ground-floor preference and disability-friendly construction features.
benefits: Priority allotment of housing units
Ground floor preference for PwD beneficiaries
Disability-friendly ramp and accessible toilet construction
Financial assistance up to ₹1.20 lakh in plains, ₹1.30 lakh in hilly areas
Free of cost under BPL category

name: Indira Gandhi National Disability Pension (IGNDPS)
description: A centrally-sponsored social pension scheme under the National Social Assistance Programme for Below Poverty Line (BPL) persons with severe or multiple disabilities aged 18–79 years.
benefits: Monthly pension of ₹300 (central share) per beneficiary
States may add their own contribution (varies by state)
Direct Benefit Transfer to bank account
No repayment required — lifelong benefit

name: NHFDC Concessional Loans for PwD
description: The National Handicapped Finance & Development Corporation offers concessional loans for self-employment, education, and skill development to PwDs through State Channelising Agencies.
benefits: Loans up to ₹30 lakh for self-employment
Educational loans up to ₹10 lakh
Interest rates as low as 5% per annum (vs 12-18% commercial rates)
Repayment period up to 7 years
Loans through State Channelising Agencies (SCAs)

name: Scholarship for Top Class Education – PwD
description: Full scholarship for PwD students admitted to prestigious institutions (IITs, IIMs, NLUs, NITs, AIIMS, etc.) for higher education. Covers tuition fees, living expenses, and more.
benefits: Full tuition fee reimbursement
Monthly maintenance allowance of ₹3,000
Book allowance of ₹3,000 per annum
Computer allowance of ₹45,000 (one-time)
Study tour / thesis reimbursement

name: Deendayal Disabled Rehabilitation Scheme (DDRS)
description: The DDRS provides grants to NGOs/VOs to run projects for education, training, and rehabilitation of PwDs including vocational training centres, special schools, and self-employment programmes.
benefits: Access to free vocational training centres
Skill development in IT, tailoring, handicrafts, and more
Career counseling and placement support
Educational support for students with disabilities
Rehabilitation services through empanelled agencies

name: National Trust Home-Based Early Intervention
description: The National Trust provides home-based early intervention services for children with autism, cerebral palsy, intellectual disabilities, and multiple disabilities from birth to 10 years.
benefits: Home visits by trained therapists
Free physiotherapy, speech therapy, and occupational therapy
Parent / caregiver training
Access to respite care facilities
Networking with specialist agencies and doctors

name: Free Railway Concession Pass for PwD
description: Indian Railways provides free travel concession to Persons with Disabilities and one escort. The concession is applicable on all classes of train travel across India.
benefits: 75% concession on rail fare for PwD
75% concession for one escort accompanying the PwD
Applicable on all train classes (Sleeper, AC, etc.)
Lower berth quota allotted priority
No income limit — available to all PwDs

name: State Disability Pension Scheme
description: State governments provide a monthly disability pension to PwDs who are below the poverty line and unable to earn livelihood due to disability. Varies by state but is available Pan-India.
benefits: Monthly pension ranging from ₹400 to ₹1,500 depending on state
Direct Benefit Transfer to bank account
No repayment required
Renewable annually through simple self-declaration
Disability certificate as the primary eligibility proof

name: Skill Development for PwD – NSTI / MSDE
description: The Ministry of Skill Development & Entrepreneurship (MSDE) and National Skill Training Institutes (NSTI) offer free short-term and long-term skill courses exclusively for PwDs across India.
benefits: Free vocational skill training in 100+ trades
Placement assistance after course completion
Stipend of ₹1,500/month during training
Certificate recognised by NCVT/SCVT
Hostel facility at NSTI campuses for outstation students

name: Ayushman Bharat PM-JAY
description: Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana covers health insurance of up to ₹5 Lakhs per family per year, including persons with primary disabilities.
benefits: Up to ₹5 Lakh per year health coverage
Cashless access to health care services
Covers up to 3 days of pre-hospitalization and 15 days post-hospitalization
Eligible across all empanelled hospitals in India

name: DISHA Scheme
description: Early intervention and School Readiness Scheme for children with Autism, Cerebral Palsy, Mental Retardation and Multiple Disabilities.
benefits: Free early intervention and daily living skills training
Rehabilitation therapy services
Pre-school education
Provision of transport and allowance

name: NRHM Disability Support
description: National Rural Health Mission supports the rehabilitation of PWDs at community level through ASHA workers and PHCs.
benefits: Community based rehabilitation services
Referrals to specialist centres
Provision of preliminary assistive therapy

name: Swavlamban Disability Insurance
description: Group Medical Insurance Scheme for Persons with Disabilities, focusing on affordability for individuals not covered by Niramaya.
benefits: Health cover up to ₹2 Lakh
Coverage against general hospitalisation
Includes OPD coverage for disability specific requirements

name: ALIMCO Assistive Devices
description: Manufacturing and distribution of subsidised prosthetics, orthotics and high-end assistive devices by Artificial Limbs Manufacturing Corporation of India.
benefits: High quality standard aids and appliances
Subsidised cost for lower income groups
Custom fits and repair services

name: RCI Rehabilitation Training
description: Training programs approved by Rehabilitation Council of India for individuals with disabilities looking for specialized skills in rehabilitation fields.
benefits: Rehabilitation professional certification
Subsidized training fee
Placement assistance
